title: file-replicator component configuration
description: The configuration file-replicator itself understands: the object at `component.global` (validated by `edgecommons component validate`), plus `$defs/instance` documenting the shape of each `component.instances[]` entry (one watched directory) for reference — mirrors `src/config.rs`'s `InstanceCfg` and `docs/reference/configuration.md`/`destinations.md`. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, streaming, credentials, parameters, health, hierarchy, identity, topic, tags) is owned by the canonical schema and is deliberately not redeclared here. Keep `additionalProperties: false` so a typo'd key is caught at deploy time rather than silently ignored.

defaults.description: Cross-instance defaults an instance inherits when it sets none of its own.

timezone.description: Default schedule timezone, used when an instance's own `schedule` sets none.

limits.description: Aggregate caps across every instance (an instance's own `limits` is a per-instance cap on top of these).

maxConcurrentFiles.description: Global in-flight-transfer semaphore size across all instances.

maxBandwidth.description: Global aggregate byte-rate cap every transfer's token bucket also passes, e.g. "50MB/s".

onPermissionError.description: Component-wide default for what happens when an instance's ingress/egress/archive/failed directory fails a startup readable/writable check. An instance's own `onPermissionError` overrides this.

permissionPolicy.description: `disableInstance` skips just the offending instance (siblings keep running) — the default. `fatal` aborts the whole component. `retain` starts the instance anyway, leaning on runtime dedup-logging + the `PermissionDenied` event for ongoing diagnostics.

retry.title: Retry / backoff policy
retry.description: Exponential backoff with full jitter, governed by a TIME BUDGET (`giveUpAfter`) rather than an attempt count — 'keep trying for 7 days' means the same thing at every backoff cadence, which is what an operator can actually reason about.

baseDelayMs.description: The first backoff window. Each attempt doubles it, up to `maxDelayMs`.

maxDelayMs.description: The backoff ceiling (default 15 min), so a long outage does not back off to next week.

giveUpAfter.description: The time budget before an item is reported exhausted, e.g. "7d". Governs long-outage tolerance (hours to ~2 days), not an attempt cap.

maxAttempts.description: Optional hard attempt cap on top of the time budget. Default: none (purely time-governed).

instance.title: One watched-directory instance
instance.description: One entry of `component.instances[]` (documented here; the CLI's schema check validates `component.global` above — this block is the reference shape for the per-instance surface, mirroring `src/config.rs`'s `InstanceCfg`).

id.description: Stable instance id. It is the `{instance}` token of this instance's UNS topics, so it must be a valid UNS token (lower-kebab) and stable across restarts (statistics, activation, and control target on it).

enabled.description: Initial activation. The persisted runtime state may override this after the first start (toggled via the activate/deactivate control message).

egress.description: Ordered destination list. `N >= 1` entries fan out independently; the source completes (delete/archive) only once EVERY entry has delivered and verified.

schedule.description: When replication runs. Default: immediate (replicate as files become ready).

limits.description: Per-instance concurrency + bandwidth caps (on top of the global caps in `component.global.limits`).

onPermissionError.description: Per-instance override of `component.global.onPermissionError`. Absent inherits the component default.

priority.description: Cross-instance GLOBAL concurrency-admission priority: under contention for the shared `component.global.limits.maxConcurrentFiles` cap, a LOWER number is admitted first (0-255-ish convention; ties broken FIFO). Governs only the global admission decision — no effect on this instance's own `limits.maxConcurrentFiles`, and no bandwidth weighting by priority.

recursive.description: Watch the whole tree; the source subtree is preserved at the destination.

include.description: Globs matched against the source-relative path. Empty means everything not excluded.

exclude.description: Globs matched against the source-relative path.

rescanSecs.description: Reconciliation-rescan interval — the fallback discovery path used when the OS file watch cannot be established or misses an event (common on NFS/SMB, container bind-mounts/overlay volumes, FUSE).

readiness.description: How the replicator decides a file is fully written. Default: stability (quietSecs: 5).

quietSecs.description: Ready once size and mtime are unchanged for this many seconds.

suffix.description: Companion marker suffix, e.g. `.done` replicates `FILE` when `FILE.done` appears.

oneOf.description: Ready only for files renamed/moved into the watch directory.

ready.description: Anything matching one of these globs (and not excluded) is ready immediately.

egress.title: One destination
egress.description: A tagged object selected by `type`. Delivery is idempotent to a stable key (a retry overwrites rather than duplicating) and is verified before the source is released, whatever the backend. Full per-backend field tables: docs/reference/destinations.md.

path.description: Destination root; the source subtree is recreated under it. Also covers NFS/SMB shares.

fsync.description: fsync the written file before completion.

privateKey.description: Path to a private key file on local disk.

hostKey.description: Pinned server host key (OpenSSH `algo base64[ comment]` line).

insecureAcceptAnyHostKey.description: Fail-open escape hatch when no `hostKey` is pinned. Disables MITM protection — use only as a documented exception.

explicitTls.description: true = explicit AUTH TLS on the plain control port; false = implicit TLS.

url.description: Base URL; the source-relative path is joined onto it.

method.description: PUT is resumable; POST is single-shot (resume forced off).

endpointUrl.description: fake-gcs-server; absent = the public GCS JSON API.

secretRef.description: An optional `{"$secret": "name"}` vault reference resolved through `gg.credentials()`. Omit to use the ambient/platform-default credential chain (backend-specific — see docs/reference/destinations.md).

expression.description: Standard cron expression, or an English sugar phrase, evaluated tz/DST-aware.

close.description: Window-close cron expression. Use this or `durationMins`.

onWindowClose.description: pauseResume: pause in-flight and resume next window if the destination supports resume, else finish current. finishCurrent: let in-flight transfers finish past close; admit no new files until the next open.

failedDir.description: Quarantine directory + `.error.json` sidecar; required when `onExhausted: quarantine`.
